rulings.Case Title: RICHARD MCLAIN et al VS. CRANE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 25, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Allan Automatic Sprinkler Corp.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Richard McLain was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant did not demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive discovery designed to elicit all the evidence they have to support their contention of liability. There is no showing that defendant propounded comprehensive special interrogatories on plaintiffs. The excerpts of Mr. Throne's deposition testimony that defendant submitted are not sufficient to demonstrate that Mr. Throne does not have knowledge that decedent was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. For these reasons, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of presenting evidence to negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. The declarations of John Rorabaugh and Robert McClain are not sufficient to meet this burden. Plaintiffs' objections to the declarations of John Rorabaugh and Robert McClain are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD MCLAIN et al VS. CRANE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Regarding Plaintiffs Negligence Claims
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 25, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Robertshaw Controls Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant owed a duty to decedent. Defendant's Separate Statement on this topic violates CRC 3.1350(d)(3). (UMF 13-14.) Regardless, plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant breached its duty owed to decedent. Even assuming that defendant was not in control of the premise(s) at issue, defendant failed to demonstrate this mandates that plaintiffs' negligence causes of action fail. Plaintiffs' objections nos. 1-8 (declaration of Daniel J. Kelly) and nos. 9-10 (declaration and deposition testimony of Robert Cantley) are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD MCLAIN et al VS. CRANE CO. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 25, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Fulton Boiler Works, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, evidence that decedent Richard McLain was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The facts stated in defendant's separate statement are not sufficient to demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive discovery designed to elicit all the evidence they have to support their contention of liability or to demonstrate that there are no persons with knowledge supporting plaintiffs' claims. Plaintiffs' objections to paragraphs 11-13 of the declaration of George Irwin are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
